# Playwright e2e fixture workspace

This directory provides a self-contained DDx workspace that the default
Playwright harness copies into a temp directory before booting the Go DDx
server. The fixture isolates e2e runs from the developer's home directory and
from the repository's live `.ddx/` state.

Contents:

- `.ddx/config.yaml` — minimal DDx config pointing at the local plugin library.
- `.ddx/beads.jsonl` — open, closed, and blocked beads so the bead API endpoints
  return non-empty data.
- `.ddx/plugins/ddx/` — minimal personas, prompts, and templates so the document
  and persona endpoints have something to list.
- `docs/` — small docs library for the document graph endpoint.

The fixture is read-only; the harness copies it to a `mktemp -d` workspace at
boot so test runs do not mutate these files. Do not reference user-home paths
from anything in this directory.

Sources section cites at least two external works.","archived_at":"2026-05-07T04:29:14Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-17T22:37:54Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1512290","created_at":"2026-04-17T22:10:38.363323602Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-13ff86c6","depends_on_id":"ddx-5241bb19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-17T22:12:23Z"}],"description":"The existing library/personas/test-engineer-tdd.md is the strongest shipped persona content-wise, but it references DDx-internal paths that only exist in the DDx repo (docs/helix/01-frame/concerns.md, cli/internal/agent/script.go, named DDx legacy offenders like orchTestGitOps/fakeExecuteBeadGit/gateTestGitOps/mockExecutor).

Rename to library/personas/test-engineer.md and generalize.

**CORE STANCES (load-bearing — preserve these):**
- Favor real e2e tests. At the e2e layer, do not mock anything — real infrastructure, real processes, real network where applicable. E2e tests that mock the database or the HTTP layer are unit tests lying about their scope.
- Stubs over mocks at the unit layer. Extract interfaces at domain boundaries; write in-memory stubs that return canned values set up by the test. Stubs do not record call sequences.
- Contract tests between in-memory stubs and real backends — same suite runs against both, proving they are interchangeable. This is the discipline that keeps stubs honest.
- Zero mocks in integration tests. Use real git (temp dir with scrubbed env), real filesystem (t.TempDir), real database (temp file / temp container), real HTTP (local test server). The only approved fake is at the AI-provider (or equivalent third-party-SDK) boundary.
- Behavior over implementation — assert on observable state, not on call counts or invocation sequences.
- Deterministic factory functions / fakers over static JSON fixtures.
- Test names describe behavior ("creates invoice when all line items valid"), not positions ("test_create_invoice_3").
- Testing pyramid as a *shape guide* (more unit than integration than e2e), not as mandated ratios.
- **Performance measurements require a baseline and clear boundaries.** Any claim that something is "fast", "scales", or "improved performance" must include: (a) a numeric baseline to compare against (prior version, prior run, published SLO), (b) an explicit boundary — what unit of work is being measured, what environment, what is excluded (cold-start, warmup, network latency, etc.), (c) reproducible harness so the measurement can be re-run. "It feels fast" and "benchmarks look good" are not performance claims.

**STRIP (cargo-cult testing dogma — do NOT ship):**
- Hard coverage percentages as universal rules (">= 80% overall", "100% on critical paths"). Replace with: "measure coverage where the project tracks it; coverage is a signal, not a target".
- Specific timing budgets as universal rules ("unit < 10ms", "integration < 100ms"). Replace with: "tests should be fast enough that developers run them; the specific threshold is a project concern".
- Specific pyramid percentages ("60% unit / 25% integration / 10% contract / 5% e2e") — keep the shape, drop the numbers.
- TDD as the only valid approach. Test-first is one workflow; test-alongside is another; test-after for a specific reason can be defensible. Do not call non-TDD "worst practice".
- Tests-for-the-sake-of-tests (mirror-of-spec tests that add no signal, trivial getters/setters).
- Mutation testing as a quality requirement. Mention as an optional signal, not a bar to clear.
- Snapshot tests without a maintenance plan.
- Retry loops around flaky tests as "improvement" — a retry loop hides a bug; fix the flake.
- Branch coverage vs line coverage dogma — say "coverage metrics matter less than whether tests catch real bugs".

**STRIP (DDx-internal specifics):**
- References to cli/internal/agent/script.go (replace with generic "a deterministic script-like fake at the AI-provider boundary")
- Named DDx legacy offenders (orchTestGitOps, fakeExecuteBeadGit, gateTestGitOps, mockExecutor) — these belong in a helix-test-engineer plugin persona, not this generic base
- Authoritative-sources frontmatter section citing docs/helix/concerns.md and HELIX concern.md
- Specific paths like cli/internal/bead, cli/internal/agent

Ground in external canon:
- Kent Beck - Test-Driven Development: By Example
- Martin Fowler - "Mocks Arent Stubs" (canonical reference for why call-sequence assertions are a code smell)
- Brendan Gregg - Systems Performance methodology (for the baseline + boundary stance on perf measurement)
- Anthropic Prompt Library (if a testing entry exists)
- TDD Guard from anthropics/skills repo (if the automated-test-first-enforcement pattern is worth mentioning)

Body structure: Philosophy / Approach / Anti-patterns / Sources.

description: Testing specialist enforcing stubs-over-mocks, contract tests, real e2e, behavior-over-implementation, testing pyramid as shape not ratios, and baselined performance measurement
tags: [testing, quality, performance]
---

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:179:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As an operator, I want a single view that shows every place my agents can run — endpoint providers and subprocess harnesses alike — with status, recent token usage, and quota utilization. When I click into one, I see a 7-day trend so I can tell if I'm about to run out.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. **Deliverable 1 — Unified view.**
   - New `harnessStatuses` GraphQL resolver returns subprocess harnesses (claude, codex, gemini, agent) with: name, kind=`harness`, reachable, detail, lastCheckedAt, defaultForProfile.
   - Existing `providerStatuses` shape gains `kind=\"endpoint\"` and `lastCheckedAt`.
   - UI page loads within 500ms on a warm cache — it renders from last-known status **first**, then patches rows live as async probes return. Playwright asserts the table is interactive within 500ms of navigation and that row status patches within 5s of the probe completing.
   - Subprocess harnesses claude and codex **appear in the table** on a machine where their binaries are installed, with correct reachable status. Integration test.

2. **Deliverable 2 — Usage + quota fields (stubbable).**
   - Per-row fields `usage` and `quota` as described. All fields nullable.
   - For endpoint providers, `usage.tokensUsedLast24h` and `usage.tokensUsedLastHour` are computed from the sessions index (fixture: ≥1k session rows covering the last 24h). Unit test asserts correctness on a seeded window.
   - For subprocess harnesses that expose rate-limit headers (claude, codex), parsed headers populate `quota.ceilingTokens`, `quota.ceilingWindowSeconds`, `quota.remaining`, `quota.resetAt`. Parser tested with captured header fixtures from each harness.
   - For harnesses/providers that expose nothing, fields remain null and the UI renders `—` / "not reported"; no fabricated data.
   - Utilization bar renders when both `usage` and `quota.ceilingTokens` are present. Sparkline renders when ≥6 hourly buckets of usage are available.

3. **Deliverable 3 — Trend detail.**
   - Detail route `/nodes/.../providers/[name]` shows 7-day and 30-day tokens/hour + requests/hour time series, with quota ceiling overlay when known.
   - Buckets computed server-side from the sessions index; the client receives ≤ 200 data points per series.
   - "Projected to hit quota in ~X" callout uses last-24h slope only. Renders only when `quota.ceilingTokens` is known and the last-24h slope is non-zero positive; otherwise absent.
   - Playwright asserts the detail route renders with seeded usage and the projection text appears for a fixture that has a ceiling + ascending usage.

4. **Perf posture.**
   - Fixture for perf test: ≥1,000 session index rows covering the last 30 days with usage distributed across ≥5 endpoints/harnesses. Per standing directive in `ddx-9ce6842a`.
   - Targets: unified list p95 ≤ 200ms HTTP (excluding async probes); trend detail p95 ≤ 400ms HTTP on the fixture.
   - If in-place aggregation cannot hit the detail-view p95 on a 10,000-row stretch fixture, pause and raise the DB-substrate decision with the user per the standing directive — do not silently switch.

5. **Design note.**
   - One-pager in `docs/helix/02-design/` before implementation recording the page-rename decision, the row model (common fields, kind-specific fields), async-probe strategy, and the quota source per harness/provider. PR links to it.

6. **No regressions.**
   - `ddx agent providers`, `ddx agent doctor`, `ddx agent list`, `ddx agent check` CLI commands continue to work unchanged.
   - `defaultRouteStatus` query remains.

7. **Cross-references.**
   - Depends on `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions index) for usage aggregation. If that bead is not yet landed when this work starts, ship Deliverable 1 standalone with usage/quota stubbed as "not available," and re-open the remaining deliverables when the index is ready.","archived_at":"2026-05-07T04:29:14Z","closing_commit_sha":"7ef7a4b1c517483485821f99aec728292bbe83a1","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:48:37.871527387Z","description":"## Observed

Three related issues on `/nodes/.../providers`:

1. **Loads slowly.** The page fires two GraphQL queries on mount — `providerStatuses` (which probes every configured provider for live connectivity) and `defaultRouteStatus` (which resolves the default route). Both are synchronous and serialized only by `Promise.all`. Each provider probe is a real network call.
2. **Missing harnesses.** The pane shows API-endpoint providers (qwen, minimax, etc. — anything wired through the service with an endpoint). It does not show subprocess-invoked harnesses like `claude` (Claude Code) or `codex`. That is accurate to the current `providerStatuses` resolver (`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_providers.go:15-50`, which calls `svc.ListProviders`) but not what an operator wants: the operator thinks of "where their agent work can run," which includes both endpoint providers AND subprocess harnesses.
3. **No quota visibility.** Operator need: see current token utilization rate + quota utilization per harness/provider, trend it, and use the trend to decide whether to throttle before hitting a cap.

## Root cause by area

- **Slow load:** `svc.ListProviders` probes every configured provider for liveness. Probes are serial within the service call, and the whole query blocks page render.
- **Missing harnesses:** Subprocess harnesses (claude, codex, gemini) are first-class in the CLI (`ddx agent list`, `ddx agent doctor`, `ddx agent check`) but are not surfaced via `providerStatuses`. They have their own status concepts — binary installed, credentials configured, rate-limit headroom — that don't map cleanly onto the endpoint-provider model.
- **No quota:** No existing data source captures rolling token usage vs quota. Quota information varies wildly by harness: Claude Code has message / session headroom concepts from response headers; codex has rate-limit headers; endpoint providers have API quota per key. There is no unified representation today.

## Proposed direction

Three deliverables, sequenced from easy to hard.

### Deliverable 1 — Unified "Agent endpoints" view (renames/rescopes the page)

- Rename the surface from "Providers" to **"Agent endpoints"** (or similar — confirm copy in the design note). The domain object is "a place where agent work can run," not "an API provider."
- Merge the data: `providerStatuses` stays as the source for endpoint providers. A new `harnessStatuses` resolver (read from `svc.ListHarnesses` / existing `ddx agent list` + `ddx agent doctor` machinery) surfaces subprocess harnesses. Page renders both in one table with a `kind` column (`endpoint` | `harness`) — or two tables if design prefers.
- Status fields common to both: name, kind, reachable (bool), detail (text), last-checked-at, default-for-profile (set of profile names).
- Asynchronous liveness. The page renders an instant first paint from cached status; liveness probes fire in background and patch rows as they return. No synchronous probe-wall on load.

### Deliverable 2 — Token & quota fields

- Extend the status payload (per row) with: `usage { tokensUsedLastHour, tokensUsedLast24h, requestsLastHour, requestsLast24h }` and `quota { ceilingTokens?, ceilingWindowSeconds?, remaining?, resetAt? }`. Any field may be null if the provider/harness doesn't expose it.
- Data sources:
  - **Endpoint providers:** aggregate from the sessions index (`ddx-2ceb02fa`) filtered to the last 1h / 24h, grouped by provider. Quota ceilings come from any headers we capture during calls, plus optional static config.
  - **Subprocess harnesses:** same sessions-index aggregation for usage. Quota — parse rate-limit headers from the harness output where available (Claude Code, codex both emit them). For harnesses that don't, leave quota null and render "not reported."
- Row gains two small inline displays: utilization bar (used / ceiling for the quota window) and a 7-day sparkline of tokens/hour.

### Deliverable 3 — Trend view

- Click into a row → detail page showing: 7-day and 30-day time-series of tokens/hour and requests/hour, with the quota ceiling overlaid. Aggregations come from the sessions index, bucketed server-side to avoid shipping raw rows.
- Simple "projected to hit quota in ~X hours/days" callout computed from the slope of the last 24h — enough to answer "are we on a trend to run out?" without building a forecasting system.

## Cross-bead dependencies

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:469:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer on the Efficacy page, I understand what Compare is for the moment I see it, and I can either select rows I already care about and compare them, or I can ignore the feature entirely if it's not for me.

**Acceptance criteria (assume outcome (2) — "redesign in place"; rewrite these if (1) or (3) is chosen):**

1. **Decision note.** One-pager in `docs/helix/02-design/` records the chosen outcome (relocate / redesign / remove), the evidence behind it (usage grep, dependency scan), and the scope of remaining work. Implementation PR links to it.

2. **Row-selection entry point.** Efficacy table rows gain a leading checkbox column. Selecting ≥2 rows enables a "Compare selected" button in the page header. With 0 or 1 rows selected the button is disabled (not hidden) with a tooltip explaining why.

3. **Pre-seeded comparison.** Clicking "Compare selected" launches the existing comparison flow with arms pre-populated from the selected rows' `(harness, provider, model)` tuples. The prompt field is still required (comparisons need a prompt) but is the only field the user has to fill in — the arm list is done.

4. **Removed from cold-start.** The standalone "Compare" button on the Efficacy page (the one that creates an empty comparison) is removed. New comparisons originate from a row selection. If users legitimately want a from-scratch comparison, that path lives on a separate `/experiments` page (out of scope for this bead unless the design note calls for it).

5. **Empty-state copy.** When the Efficacy table is empty, the Compare affordance is hidden entirely (no point selecting zero rows).

6. **Playwright e2e.**
   - Seeds the fixture with ≥3 efficacy rows.
   - Asserts the Compare button is disabled with 0/1 selections and enabled with 2+.
   - Selects 2 rows, clicks Compare, asserts the comparison form opens with arms pre-populated from those rows and only the prompt field is empty.

7. **No regression to efficacy perf.** All perf targets from `ddx-0a33bc5f` still hold. Adding a checkbox column does not change the payload the resolver returns.","archived_at":"2026-05-07T04:29:14Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-24T06:34:30Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"196235","closing_commit_sha":"7db5cc9c31bb89b506ba5716148976f904db2aba","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:47:38.842277927Z","description":"## Observed

The Efficacy page has a "Compare" button that opens a flow for adding "arms" and writing a prompt. User feedback: they're not sure what to do with it. It does not match their mental model of what efficacy is (a session aggregation grouped by provider / harness / model).

## Ground truth

- The backing feature is `Query.comparisons` and the comparison record set (`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:211-226`). It's an A/B-style "run the same prompt across N arms and compare results" tool. That is a real capability — just not what a rollup/efficacy dashboard is for.
- It's colocated with Efficacy today probably because both involve harness/model performance. The colocation is creating confusion: the Efficacy page promises "how are things doing" and the Compare button promises "design an experiment" — different audiences, different tasks.

## Decision required

Before implementation, decide one of three:

1. **Relocate.** Move Compare to its own top-level surface (e.g., `/experiments` or `/compare`) and cleanly separate "how did things go" (efficacy) from "design an A/B" (compare). Efficacy page loses the Compare button entirely.
2. **Redesign in place.** Keep it on the Efficacy page but recast as "Compare these rows" — the user selects 2+ efficacy rows and launches a comparison pre-seeded from them. Removes the cold-start prompt-entry UX; connects directly to the rollup the user is already looking at.
3. **Remove.** If comparisons are not being used by real workflows, drop the feature and the associated UI/resolver. Least code, least surface area.

The user did not mandate a specific choice — they flagged the confusion and the mental model. Option 2 aligns best with their stated mental model (efficacy = session aggregation); option 1 is the cleanest separation of concerns; option 3 is viable only if usage data supports it.

## Proposed direction

Run the design decision as a short spike (≤ half a day):

- Check whether `comparisons` is referenced by anything beyond this UI page: CLI, docs, specs, other server endpoints. Deletion cost is a function of that.
- Usage check: grep agent-logs + bead evidence for any `comparison_id` or `compare` correlation over the last 30 days. Is anyone actually running comparisons?
- Write a one-page decision note in `docs/helix/02-design/` recording the choice, the evidence that supports it, and the scope of the implementation work.

This bead's remaining acceptance criteria are written assuming the outcome is (2) "redesign in place" — the most-likely-to-be-built option. They should be rewritten when the decision is made if the outcome is (1) or (3).

## Out of scope

- Anything the Efficacy perf/source rework (`ddx-0a33bc5f`) covers.
- Multi-run orchestration changes to how comparisons execute.","events_attachment":"ddx-6ce6d7a7/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-24T06:39:30.288516189Z","execute-loop-last-detail":"escalation exhausted: unsupported model "minimax/minimax-m2.7" for harness "gemini"; supported models: gemini-2.5-pro, gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.5-flash-lite","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","execute-loop-retry-after":"2026-04-23T03:02:04Z","feature":"FEAT-019","id":"ddx-6ce6d7a7","issue_type":"task","labels":["feat-008","feat-019","efficacy","ui","design"],"owner":"ddx","priority":3,"session_id":"eb-1ea62cda","status":"closed","title":"Efficacy 'Compare arms' button is unclear and misplaced: decide whether to relocate, redesign, or remove","updated_at":"2026-04-24T06:40:37.909246767Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:516:{"archived_at":"2026-05-07T04:29:14Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-15T06:24:35Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3766264","closing_commit_sha":"604f17d53ce26b5776472f9fa77a82a2eb1b9cba","created_at":"2026-04-15T01:35:08.058474818Z","description":"The ddx-bead skill guides bead creation but underspecifies what makes a bead *execution-ready* for automated execute-bead runs. Observed during axon ADR-018 execute-loop driving: beads that technically met the current skill's requirements (title, AC, labels, description pointing at spec-id) still produced bad execute-bead outcomes because the description lacked the context the agent needed to ship correct code in one pass.

## Gaps in the current skill

1. **Description quality is underspecified.** The skill gives AC quality rules but the description field just says 'per SD-004 Section 2' is OK. In practice, agents need:
   - Exact file paths to touch (e.g., 'crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs')
   - Cross-bead context when dependencies are listed (e.g., 'A1 landed the credential_revocations table in crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs — query it via SELECT 1')
   - Inline spec excerpts, NOT just doc-id references. If the spec is ADR-018 §4's failure table, paste the table into the description. Don't say 'see ADR-018 §4'.
   - Files NOT to touch (scope negation)

2. **spec-id and the doc graph are unreliable for execution context.** The current skill implies spec-id linkage is sufficient. It is not — spec-id drives docgraph.BuildGraphWithConfig() which only resolves HELIX-shaped doc references. Generic beads on non-HELIX projects have no equivalent. AND even when resolution works, the agent only gets a file path, not the relevant section. The bead description is the only place that carries content the agent can rely on.

3. **No guidance on cross-bead context.** When bead B depends on bead A, B's description should describe what A landed (file paths, type names, key APIs) — not assume the agent will reopen A's history. Automated execute-bead does not have that context; each bead is read cold.

4. **No guidance on verification commands in AC.** AC items like 'unit tests green' are too vague. Should be 'cargo test -p axon-server green' or 'bun test passes in sdk/typescript'. Named commands.

5. **No guidance on bead size.** A bead should be one unit of testable work, sized so a single agent pass can complete it. Too small is overhead; too large defeats review. Heuristic: if the AC lists more than ~12 checkboxes, consider splitting.

6. **No guidance on incorporating review findings on reopen.** When a bead is reopened after a REQUEST_CHANGES review, the author should either (a) fold the review findings into the description for the next attempt to see, or (b) rely on the tool to thread review events into the prompt (a separate ddx bead — see ddx-a317208c). The skill is silent on this.

7. **No template for description.** A recommended description template (Scope / Files to touch / Cross-bead context / Verification / Quality bar) would make good beads cheaper to author.

## Scope

Update skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md to add:

- A 'What makes a bead execution-ready' section that codifies the points above
- A 'Description template' with recommended sections: Scope, Files to touch, Files NOT to touch, Cross-bead context, Verification commands, Spec excerpts inline
- A 'Bead sizing' section with the ~12-AC heuristic
- A 'Review-driven reopen' section that tells authors to update the description in place when a bead is reopened after a review rejection
- A 'Common anti-patterns' section enumerating the mistakes observed during the axon execute-loop:
  - Listing doc IDs without inlining the relevant text
  - Not naming file paths in the description
  - AC items that are not verifiable by a named command
  - Depending on unmerged work without explaining what it landed

Keep the existing required-fields and label-conventions sections — they are correct.

## Context

Observed during axon ADR-018 execute-loop:
- Bead A1 was well-sized but initially had scope creep in its description (audit_retention_policies, which belonged to F1). Agent added the scope-creep table faithfully.
- Bead A2's initial description said 'UserIdentity { provider, subject, user_id }' — a typo carried over from an earlier ADR revision. Agent implemented 'subject' faithfully even though ADR-018 §3 said 'external_id'. The description was the contract the agent followed.
- Bead B1 is being re-authored right now with a richer description (inline ADR-018 §4 failure table, exact file paths, cross-bead context about A1 schema and A2 types) — this is the experiment to validate the authoring changes proposed in this bead.

## Acceptance

- [ ] skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md gains 'What makes a bead execution-ready' section
- [ ] Description template added with the 6 recommended sections
- [ ] Bead sizing heuristic documented
- [ ] Review-driven reopen guidance added
- [ ] Common anti-patterns enumerated
- [ ] Skill still lints against any existing skill-format CI check","events_attachment":"ddx-78613f54/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-15T06:25:35.769449241Z","id":"ddx-78613f54","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","area:bead","area:skill","kind:documentation","gap:bead-authoring"],"owner":"ddx","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-4ef43076","status":"closed","title":"skill: improve ddx-bead SKILL.md with authoring discipline for execution-ready beads","updated_at":"2026-04-15T06:25:42.623027765Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:723:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer browsing the web UI beads view, I want clicking a bead to open its detail pane within ~200ms on a repo with hundreds of beads across several projects, so the UI feels interactive.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. A benchmark/harness exists (Go benchmark or scripted GraphQL client) that measures end-to-end `bead(id:)` latency against a fixture repo with ≥ 5 projects and ≥ 500 beads total. Output is a p50/p95 number, not a pass/fail bool.
2. Baseline is recorded in the bead notes (actual number measured on the current `main` binary at the time of fix).
3. After the fix, p95 for `bead(id:)` on the fixture is ≤ 50ms in-process / ≤ 200ms over HTTP on a dev laptop.
4. The fix does not introduce a stale-read regression: writing a bead via `ddx bead update` and then fetching it via GraphQL returns the new value on the next request (verified by a test that does write-then-read).
5. Concurrency-safe: an integration test spawns N concurrent `bead(id:)` queries interleaved with `bead update` mutations and asserts no panics, no torn reads, and consistent responses.
6. Playwright e2e: open `/nodes/.../beads`, click a bead row, assert the sidepane shows the bead title within 1s (generous budget; guards against regressions not the p95).","archived_at":"2026-05-07T04:29:14Z","closing_commit_sha":"c1822352b4bbfa4181f96a203bce522d249f342d","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:07:18.806879855Z","description":"## Observed

Opening a bead in the sidepane on `/nodes/.../projects/.../beads?q=test` takes 5+ seconds. The beads list renders quickly; the delay is on single-bead lookup.

## Root cause

`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver.go:93-100` — the `Bead(id)` resolver calls `r.State.GetBeadSnapshots("", "", "", "")` with no filters, which at `cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:49-105` walks **every project**, constructs a `bead.Store` per project, calls `ReadAll()` (full directory scan + JSONL parse of every bead file), then iterates the result in Go to find one matching ID.

So every sidepane open = `O(projects × beads_per_project)` disk reads + parses, for a lookup that should be `O(1)` or `O(log n)`.

`bead.Store` already exposes `Get(id)` (`cli/internal/bead/store.go:450`), but the resolver doesn't use it — nor does it know which project the bead lives in, because the GraphQL schema exposes `bead(id:)` without a project scope.

## Proposed direction (not prescriptive)

Any of these would resolve it; pick based on measurements:

1. **Project scope the query.** Change schema to `bead(projectId:, id:)` (or accept a composite global id) and call `Store.Get` directly.
2. **Maintain an in-memory index** `beadID → projectID` refreshed on bead mutations and on a debounced filesystem watcher; resolver does one map lookup + one `Store.Get`.
3. **Short-TTL snapshot cache** behind `GetBeadSnapshots`, invalidated on bead writes.

Before picking: add a baseline measurement harness (see acceptance) so we know the actual p50/p95 before and after, not just anecdote.","events_attachment":"ddx-ad0db8fd/events.jsonl","execute-loop-last-detail":"escalation exhausted: unsupported model "minimax/minimax-m2.7" for harness "gemini"; supported models: gemini-2.5-pro, gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.5-flash-lite","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"feature":"FEAT-008","id":"ddx-ad0db8fd","issue_type":"bug","labels":["feat-008","feat-004","perf","graphql"],"notes":"Baseline/after measurement for bead(id:) lookup fixture (5 projects, 500 total beads, go test ./internal/server -run ^$ -bench "BenchmarkGraphQLBeadLookup(LegacyScan)?Latency$" -benchtime=80x -count=1, linux/arm64):

README or docs/ entry describes how to regenerate fixtures via scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh when omlx server behavior changes.","archived_at":"2026-05-07T04:29:14Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-18T04:14:07Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1833233","closing_commit_sha":"ad14ee84ff0faa5b8754f5274ea795fb1511cde7","created_at":"2026-04-18T03:01:36.207143767Z","description":"## Context

ddx-agent v0.3.14 shipped the SSE comment-frame filter that unblocks omlx. Manual dogfooding (2026-04-18) confirmed a single bead executing via vidar-omlx for 6+ minutes with 17,000+ streaming events before the fix caused the regression this bead is meant to prevent: we have no automated assertion that the omlx path actually stays healthy.

Both fresh-eyes reviews (codex + opus, 2026-04-18) flagged: 'missing bead for v0.3.14 consumer-side smoke test in DDx'. This is that bead.

## Fix

Add an end-to-end smoke test that runs a real ddx work invocation against a recorded-or-live omlx endpoint and asserts the attempt closes (or at least produces a closing-status result: success / already_satisfied / a genuine model-side error) without surfacing 'unexpected end of JSON input' or silent stream termination.

Two implementation options:

1. **Record + replay via virtual harness** (preferred for CI isolation). Capture a full AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1 trace of a successful vidar-omlx run, store it as a fixture, replay via a test double that emulates omlx's SSE quirks (keep-alive comment frames, reasoning warmup silence, reasoning_content deltas). Test asserts ddx-agent SDK consumes the full stream, ddx work's execute-bead orchestrator records closing status, and bead state mutates correctly.

2. **Live smoke, opt-in** (developer-machine only). A test file guarded by build tag  that makes a real request to vidar-omlx. Useful for catching provider-side regressions (e.g., omlx server ships a breaking change). Skip in CI.

Do BOTH: option 1 for CI signal, option 2 for manual release verification.

## What this prevents

- Regression: a future ddx-agent bump reintroduces the SSE-comment-frame bug or similar streaming defect. CI fixture test catches it before the bump merges.
- Regression: DDx-side change breaks the omlx dispatch path (e.g., adds a header that trips omlx's parser). CI catches it.
- Silent-success: ddx work completes but actually landed a bogus result_rev because of a subtle SSE-frame-dropping bug. Fixture replay asserts the expected final outcome.

## Coverage target

Minimum: one happy-path fixture (successful bead close) AND one error-path fixture (model returns malformed tool-call; execute-bead records task_failed cleanly without corrupting the bead tracker).

## Files likely touched

- cli/internal/agent/omlx_e2e_test.go (new) — fixture replay + assertions
- testdata/omlx-wire/ (new) — captured AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE JSONL fixtures
- scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh (new) — helper to regenerate fixtures against a live endpoint

## Depends on

- agent-941e7e42 (AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1) shipped in v0.3.14 — needed to capture full fixtures.","events_attachment":"ddx-bbb2d177/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-18T04:30:37.397634223Z","id":"ddx-bbb2d177","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","phase:test","kind:testing","area:agent","area:omlx","goal:throughput-per-dollar"],"owner":"erik","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-a32f1082","spec-id":"FEAT-006","status":"closed","title":"eval: v0.3.14 consumer-side end-to-end smoke — ddx work via vidar-omlx closes one bead","updated_at":"2026-04-18T04:31:47.76623379Z"}

4. Bead routing event records: requested_profile, requested_tier, resolved_tier, escalation_count, final_tier. A run that escalates cheap -> standard -> success at standard has an event with escalation_count=1, resolved_tier=standard, final_tier=standard.

FEAT-006 Profile Routing section updated with the ladder semantics and the intent table (default/cheap/fast/smart).","archived_at":"2026-05-07T04:29:14Z","closing_commit_sha":"93f938c41c93be78fc199bd150f455add6575d24","created_at":"2026-04-18T02:51:49.512908486Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-bbb65768","depends_on_id":"ddx-2d974641","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T02:52:01Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-bbb65768","depends_on_id":"ddx-e7254128","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-20T13:58:09Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-bbb65768","depends_on_id":"ddx-7bc0c8d5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-20T14:36:21Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-bbb65768","depends_on_id":"ddx-ea7f041c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-21T14:36:16Z"}],"description":"## Target UX

The user-facing profile vocabulary (cheap / default / fast / smart) should map to concrete routing policies that take full advantage of local models without blocking on their limits. Specifically:

### default profile (the common case)
Try local models first, escalate to cloud if:
- local model's context window is too small for the bead prompt
- local model lacks a capability the bead requires (tools, structured output, etc.)
- local dispatch fails (provider unreachable, timeout, model-side error)
- bead reopens due to post-run check failure (review-malfunction, land-conflict, etc.)

Escalation target: next tier up in the ladder (standard cloud — e.g. codex gpt-5.4 within subscription). If THAT fails, escalate once more to smart cloud. Then stop and unclaim.

### cheap profile
Local only. Never escalate. Intended for high-volume exploratory work where cost dominates and adequate-quality is sufficient. If the cheap tier can't serve the bead, the bead unclaims and waits.

### fast profile
Cloud-first, skip local entirely. Intended for latency-critical or interactive work where local inference's 9s warmup is unacceptable. Starts at a fast cloud model (e.g. gemini-flash or haiku) and escalates to smart on failure.

### smart profile
Cloud-high-quality from the start. Intended for bead-review, adversarial checks, or beads whose AC demands high-quality reasoning. No escalation — if smart fails, the work fails meaningfully.

## Why this matters (dogfooding signal)

As of 2026-04-18, DDx's default is cheap = qwen/qwen3.6 local (vidar-omlx). With v0.3.14 shipping the SSE-comment-frame filter, the local path actually works end-to-end. The queue can drain mostly on local hardware for pennies per bead, escalating only when the bead genuinely needs cloud quality. That's the throughput-per-dollar goal (goal:throughput-per-dollar label).

## Prereqs (already filed under ddx-2d974641 or sibling beads)

This epic is blocked on the autorouting epic delivering its exit-gate UX — 'declare provider + use canonical ref + autorouting works'. Specifically:

- ddx-8610020e (RouteRequest.Provider field) — foundation
- ddx-0486e601 (fuzzy normalization + per-provider) — lets cheap-tier model refs resolve to provider-native IDs
- ddx-2f5a2284 (catalog/Profile discovery integration) — lets Profile tier overrides retarget to provider-native IDs
- ddx-3c5ba7cc (tier escalation --provider affinity) — the lower half of this epic's 'escalate on failure' semantics
- ddx-4817edfd (capability gating) — prevents routing to a model that can't serve the bead

With those in place, the profile-level policy here is mostly CONFIGURATION + a small amount of scoring-layer logic.

## Scope

### Config schema additions

.ddx/config.yaml agent.routing extends with an explicit per-profile ladder:

    agent:
      routing:
        profile_ladders:
          default: [cheap, standard, smart]      # local first, escalate
          cheap:   [cheap]                        # local only
          fast:    [fast, smart]                  # cloud fast, escalate to smart
          smart:   [smart]                        # cloud high-quality, no escalation
        model_overrides:
          cheap:    qwen/qwen3.6
          fast:     kimi/k2.5
          standard: codex/gpt-5.4                # new: subscription cloud, mid-quality
          smart:    minimax/minimax-m2.7

Current profile_priority field (a flat list) becomes a legacy alias for profile_ladders.default with a deprecation warning.

### Tier-escalation policy (routing.go)

For a given profile P, iterate profile_ladders[P]:
- Evaluate model_overrides[tier] resolves on a viable candidate (with --provider affinity from ddx-3c5ba7cc).
- If yes, try it; on failure (execution_failed / land_conflict / post_run_check_failed), move to next tier.
- Record tier decision + escalation chain in routing event on the bead so telemetry reflects where work actually landed.

### User-facing CLI

- ddx work --profile default — the new primary invocation. Most users run this.
- ddx work --profile cheap / fast / smart — opt-in deviations.
- --max-tier continues to cap the ladder (explicit user override beats config).

### Telemetry + measurement

Every execution event records: requested_profile, requested_tier, resolved_tier, escalation_count, final_tier. Aggregated metrics (FEAT-016 / ddx-4b59b816) report per-profile throughput, per-tier cost, per-bead escalation rate.

## Exit gate

'ddx work --profile default --once --local' against a ready bead on a host with a healthy local provider AND healthy cloud fallback:
- Claims the bead.
- Tries cheap tier (local). If it succeeds, closes bead with tier=cheap recorded.
- If cheap fails for any escalation-triggering reason, automatically retries at standard tier without operator intervention.
- If standard fails, retries at smart.
- Final outcome recorded faithfully (success at whatever tier landed, or exhaustion if all tiers failed).

## Out of scope

- Dynamic profile selection ('auto-pick profile based on bead complexity'). Too much magic; explicit profile choice is fine.
- Cost budgets / circuit breakers. FEAT-014 (token awareness) tracks this separately.
- Per-bead profile overrides (bead-level 'requires-profile: smart'). Could be a future follow-up but out of scope here.

## Metadata

Governing spec: FEAT-006 (agent service) + the autorouting epic ddx-2d974641. This bead is the PROFILE-layer policy that sits on top of the routing fixes from ddx-2d974641.   Performance analysis across model / prompt / harness / spec
  dimensions today requires fan-out reads of every bead's
  events.jsonl. There is no global per-attempt table with
  denormalized fields ready for query. After sister ADR formalizes
  the storage architecture, this bead implements the cold-tier
  metrics extraction.

  Without this, optimization is blind: we cannot answer "which
  model has the best cost-per-success for our drain" or "which
  prompt template fails most on which spec class."

CURRENT STATE
  - .ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl contains lifecycle
    events: routing, cost, review-pass, review-block,
    closed-merged, etc. These rows have the raw signals
    (model, harness, cost_usd, tokens, verdict) needed for
    aggregate analysis.
  - There is no global attempts.jsonl file aggregating across
    beads.
  - Sister beads B29 (retention) cannot safely prune local
    evidence until metrics have been captured.

PROPOSED FIX
  1. Define schema for .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl (one row per
     bead-attempt; JSONL, append-only):
        - schema_version: 1 (integer; for future migration)
        - attempt_id: string (current "run-id" until rename
          bead lands, then attempt-id)
        - bead_id: string
        - session_id: string (agent harness session)
        - ts_start, ts_end: RFC3339 timestamps
        - model: string
        - harness: string
        - profile: string (optional)
        - provider: string
        - prompt_template_id: string (optional)
        - prompt_template_hash: string (hash of the rendered
          prompt; enables grouping by prompt variant)
        - spec_id: string (FEAT-N, SD-N, TD-N, ADR-N if known)
        - outcome: string (success | execution_failed |
          review_block | review_fixable_gap | etc.; see TD-031 §5)
        - exit_code: integer
        - cost_usd: float
        - duration_ms: integer
        - input_tokens, output_tokens, total_tokens: integer
        - review_verdict: string (APPROVE | BLOCK | etc.;
          optional)
        - review_finding_count_by_severity: map of
          severity -> count (optional)
        - ladder_min_power_initial, ladder_min_power_final:
          integer (per ADR-024 ladder semantics)
        - ladder_steps_taken: integer

  2. Forward-going hook:
     - On attempt finalization in
       cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (or sibling),
       extract the fields from result.json + manifest.json + the
       attempt's events and append a row to
       .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl.
     - Use O_APPEND for atomic single-writer appends.
     - On multi-drain machines (future federation): partition
       to .ddx/metrics/<machine-id>/attempts.jsonl (sister bead
       deferred until federation arrives).

  3. One-shot backfill:
     - cli/cmd/work_metrics_backfill.go (new file) implements
       ddx work metrics backfill.
     - For each closed bead, read its events.jsonl (in
       .ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl or inline in
       beads.jsonl) and emit one row per attempt found.
     - DO NOT read .ddx/executions/<dir>/ for the backfill;
       events.jsonl is sufficient.
     - Idempotent: skip rows already in attempts.jsonl
       (dedupe by attempt_id).

  4. Tests:
     - TestMetrics_AppendRowOnFinalization: assert a row is
       added per attempt with all expected fields populated.
     - TestMetrics_BackfillFromEvents: assert backfill emits
       correct rows from existing events.
     - TestMetrics_BackfillIdempotent: assert re-running
       backfill does not duplicate rows.
     - TestMetrics_SchemaVersion: assert schema_version field
       is present and equals 1.

NON-SCOPE
  - The analyze CLI (sister bead).
  - Multi-machine partitioning (deferred until federation).
  - Adding agent-log-derived fields (tool_call_count, etc.) —
    those can be added later via schema_version bump.

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead "docs(ADR): formalize 3-tier evidence storage"
    — this bead implements the cold-tier metrics layer the ADR
    documents.
  - Sister bead B29 (RetainDays prune) DEPENDS on this bead
    landing first; pruning local evidence is safe only after
    metrics are extracted.
  - Sister bead "feat(work): ddx work analyze CLI" reads
    .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl.","events_attachment":"ddx-054b2449/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-05-10T22:15:37.75491886Z","id":"ddx-054b2449","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:6","area:agent","area:metrics","kind:feature","spec:ADR"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"queue-rank":"33","session_id":"eb-d966d859","status":"closed","title":"feat(metrics): extract per-attempt rows to .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl","updated_at":"2026-05-10T23:21:30.585860255Z"}

When a pre-land reviewer finds a fixable implementation or test gap, DDx should repair in the same still-live attempt worktree instead of landing a rejected candidate or starting from a cold worktree. No same-worktree repair loop exists today.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review.go:233-244 records REQUEST_CHANGES as a non-approved review outcome but does not launch an implementer repair pass.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1481-1487 includes a review gate prompt block, but the normal implementation prompt also says commit exactly once at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1520-1523 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1550-1555; repair needs a distinct prompt mode that permits one append-only repair commit.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1086-1123 counts review outcomes but has no loop that appends repair commits and re-runs checks/review before final land.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a RepairPass that runs in the same attempt worktree when ReviewFindingClassifier returns review_fixable_gap.
- Build a repair prompt that includes prior review findings, per-AC failures, failed candidate_rev, base_rev, required verification commands, and explicit append-only commit rules.
- Permit one repair commit per repair cycle while forbidding reset, amend, squash, rebase, or history rewrite.
- After each repair commit, pin a new candidate ref, re-run checks, re-run review against base_rev..new_candidate_rev, and stop on approval, manual class, repair_max_cycles, no-progress, provider/cost budget, or context cancellation.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not enable two-slot review; this uses the transitional single-slot review path.
- Do not implement concrete model fallback; routing power floors are a separate bead.

DEPS
Depends on classification so only review_fixable_gap enters repair.","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-05-09T04:35:37.478860602Z","id":"ddx-2471b63d","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","area:agent","area:review","kind:feature","reliability","adr:024","spec:FEAT-010","candidate-cycle","repair"],"owner":"erik","parent":"ddx-31e07fdc","priority":0,"spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"closed","title":"repair: run same-worktree append-only repair cycles","updated_at":"2026-05-09T04:43:25.350373076Z"}

The server documents resolver rebuilds the full artifact graph on every page request, even when the underlying graph has not changed. On a 2000-artifact corpus, this produces visible latency on every load and makes concurrent requests redundantly expensive.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/graphql_resolver.go (or equivalent resolver file): the documents resolver calls the full graph-build pipeline on every GraphQL request without checking if the graph revision has changed since the last build.
- No normalized artifact cache (by graph revision) exists — every request starts cold.
- The search path in the documents resolver also rebuilds the full artifact list on every search call.
- ddx-5681cc57 (2k fixture) is a dep: cache design must be validated against the 2k scale fixture.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a cache keyed by graph revision (mtime-based, as recommended in the perf epic): store normalized artifact list (lowercased title/path/id/mediaType) keyed by revision string.
- Add search to documents resolver: filter the cached normalized list by search query.
- Add invalidation on file change: detect mtime changes and invalidate cache entry.
- Ensure concurrent requests don't double-build (single-flight pattern or sync.Once per revision).

NON-SCOPE
- Caching for resolvers other than documents.
- CI performance gate (that is a later bead).","id":"ddx-4a7eed8c","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","story:7","area:server","kind:feature"],"parent":"ddx-97335425","priority":2,"status":"open","title":"perf: cache graph/artifact metadata + add search to documents resolver + invalidation","updated_at":"2026-05-04T16:17:46.692603922Z"}

.ddx/beads.jsonl:228:{"acceptance":"1. cli/cmd/bead_replay.go (or sibling) implements `ddx bead replay <attempt-id>` with --harness, --model, --profile, --min-power, --prompt-from, --rev override flags.
2. Replay loads the original attempt's manifest.json and prompt.md from .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ (with fallback to mirror per ddx-b2c6ae22).
3. Replay runs in one-shot mode: emits a "replay" event with the source attempt-id, does NOT count toward the bead's drain-attempt history.
4. A new evidence bundle is created at .ddx/executions/<new-run-id>/ with manifest noting replay_of=<original>.
5. A metrics row is appended to .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl with replay_of=<original-attempt-id> for grouping.
6. Replay prints a side-by-side comparison of original vs replay metrics.
7. `ddx bead replay-bench <bead-id> --variants <list>` runs multiple replays in parallel and outputs an outcome matrix.
8. TestReplay_LoadsOriginalManifest, TestReplay_OverridesAppliedToDispatch, TestReplay_DoesNotCountTowardBeadAttemptHistory, TestReplay_AppendsMetricsRowWithReplayOf, TestReplay_PrintsComparison, TestReplayBench_MultipleVariantsInParallel all pass.
9. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green.
10. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","claimed-at":"2026-05-11T02:25:57Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2741772","closing_commit_sha":"303324f44868371fe306d29f0547726eaa525eed","created_at":"2026-05-11T00:55:59.156493367Z","description":"PROBLEM
  The metrics file (.ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl, ddx-054b2449)
  and analyze CLI (ddx-09ceb430) reveal which models / prompts
  / harnesses succeed at what rate. But they cannot answer
  "would this specific attempt have succeeded with a different
  configuration?" That question matters for prompt iteration
  and model comparison — A/B testing on real beads, not
  synthetic fixtures.

  Currently the only way to re-run a bead is to reopen it and
  let the drain claim it again with whatever default config
  is in effect. There's no controlled replay against alternate
  config.

CURRENT STATE
  - cli/cmd/agent_run.go runs a fresh execution with the
    configured harness/model.
  - .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ contains the prompt.md and
    manifest.json that defined the original execution.
  - There is no `ddx bead replay` or equivalent.
  - For prompt-template iteration, operators currently must
    manually copy prompts and run them ad-hoc — no integration
    with bead state, no metrics row, no comparison view.

PROPOSED FIX
  Implement `ddx bead replay <attempt-id> [flags]`:

  1. Load the original attempt's manifest.json and prompt.md
     from .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ (or the mirror, per
     ddx-b2c6ae22).

  2. Construct a new dispatch request with overrides from
     flags:
       --harness <name>          override the original harness
       --model <name>            override the original model
       --profile <name>          override the original profile
       --min-power <n>           override the routing floor
       --prompt-from <path>      override the prompt content
                                 (substitute prompt-template
                                 iteration for the original)
       --rev <ref>               override the base revision

  3. Run the dispatch in a one-shot mode (does NOT count toward
     the bead's drain-attempt history; emits a "replay" event
     with the original attempt_id and the new run-id).

  4. Capture a fresh evidence bundle under
     .ddx/executions/<new-run-id>/ tagged with
     manifest.json containing both the new and the
     original-source attempt-id for comparison.

  5. Append a row to .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl with a new
     attempt_id, marked replay_of=<original-attempt-id> so the
     analyze CLI can group replays.

  6. Print a side-by-side comparison:
     - original: outcome, cost, duration, tool_call_count
     - replay:   outcome, cost, duration, tool_call_count
     - delta:    relative metrics

  CLI sub-mode: `ddx bead replay-bench <bead-id>
  --variants <var1>,<var2>,...` runs multiple replays in
  parallel against the same source attempt with different
  configurations. Output is a matrix of outcomes for A/B
  analysis.

  Tests:
    - TestReplay_LoadsOriginalManifest
    - TestReplay_OverridesAppliedToDispatch
    - TestReplay_DoesNotCountTowardBeadAttemptHistory
    - TestReplay_AppendsMetricsRowWithReplayOf
    - TestReplay_PrintsComparison
    - TestReplayBench_MultipleVariantsInParallel

NON-SCOPE
  - UI / dashboard for replay comparison.
  - Auto-selecting replay configurations based on metrics
    patterns (would be valuable; out of scope here).
  - Cross-machine replay (requires the warm mirror to be
    configured).

INTERSECTIONS
  - DEPENDS ON: ddx-054b2449 (metrics extraction) — replay rows
    extend the same schema.
  - Depends loosely on ddx-b2c6ae22 (mirror) — for replaying
    attempts whose local evidence has been pruned.
  - Sister bead ddx-09ceb430 (analyze CLI) — gains awareness
    of replay rows via replay_of grouping.","events_attachment":"ddx-a0b2441c/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-05-11T02:25:57.692454506Z","id":"ddx-a0b2441c","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:6","area:agent","area:bead","kind:feature","observability"],"owner":"erik","priority":1,"queue-rank":"40","session_id":"eb-1980a3f8","status":"closed","title":"feat(bead): ddx bead replay — re-execute an attempt against alternate model/harness/prompt","updated_at":"2026-05-11T02:43:17.41898763Z"}

The first\n+// line is the {\"kind\":\"meta\", ...} record; remaining {\"kind\":\"frame\",\"body\":...}\n+// lines are the SSE bodies to stream in order.\n+func loadOMLXFixture(t *testing.T, path string) omlxFixture {\n+\tt.Helper()\n+\tf, err := os.Open(path) // #nosec G304 -- test fixtures are checked in.\n+\trequire.NoError(t, err, \"open fixture %s\", path)\n+\tt.Cleanup(func() { _ = f.Close() })\n+\n+\tvar fix omlxFixture\n+\tscanner := bufio.NewScanner(f)\n+\t// Default 64KB scanner buffer is tight for long reasoning lines.\n+\tscanner.Buffer(make([]byte, 64*1024), 1024*1024)\n+\tfirst := true\n+\tfor scanner.Scan() {\n+\t\tline := strings.TrimSpace(scanner.Text())\n+\t\tif line == \"\" {\n+\t\t\tcontinue\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tvar record struct {\n+\t\t\tKind string          `json:\"kind\"`\n+\t\t\tBody string          `json:\"body\"`\n+\t\t\tRaw  json.RawMessage `json:\"-\"`\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &record); err != nil {\n+\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"parsing fixture line in %s: %v\", path, err)\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tswitch record.Kind {\n+\t\tcase \"meta\":\n+\t\t\tif !first {\n+\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"fixture %s: meta record must be the first non-empty line\", path)\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &fix.Meta); err != nil {\n+\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"parsing meta in %s: %v\", path, err)\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\tcase \"frame\":\n+\t\t\tfix.Frames = append(fix.Frames, record.Body)\n+\t\tdefault:\n+\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"fixture %s: unknown record kind %q\", path, record.Kind)\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tfirst = false\n+\t}\n+\trequire.NoError(t, scanner.Err(), \"scanning fixture %s\", path)\n+\trequire.NotEmpty(t, fix.Meta.Name, \"fixture %s: missing meta.name\", path)\n+\trequire.NotEmpty(t, fix.Frames, \"fixture %s: no frame records\", path)\n+\treturn fix\n+}\n+\n+// newOMLXReplayServer stands up an httptest.Server that behaves like a vidar-omlx\n+// /v1/chat/completions endpoint by replaying the fixture's frames verbatim,\n+// with a short inter-frame flush so the consumer's SSE decoder sees per-chunk\n+// arrivals rather than one batched blob.\n+func newOMLXReplayServer(t *testing.T, frames []string) *httptest.Server {\n+\tt.Helper()\n+\tsrv := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n+\t\t// Match omlx's advertised Content-Type exactly — the SSE comment-frame\n+\t\t// middleware in provider/openai activates only when Content-Type starts\n+\t\t// with text/event-stream.\n+\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"text/event-stream; charset=utf-8\")\n+\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Cache-Control\", \"no-cache\")\n+\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Transfer-Encoding\", \"chunked\")\n+\t\tflusher, _ := w.(http.Flusher)\n+\t\tfor _, body := range frames {\n+\t\t\tif _, err := io.WriteString(w, body); err != nil {\n+\t\t\t\treturn\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\t\tif flusher != nil {\n+\t\t\t\tflusher.Flush()\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\t\t// Small delay to reproduce per-frame arrival; long enough that the\n+\t\t\t// decoder cannot batch frames, short enough that the test stays\n+\t\t\t// under a second total.\n+\t\t\ttime.Sleep(5 * time.Millisecond)\n+\t\t}\n+\t}))\n+\tt.Cleanup(srv.Close)\n+\treturn srv\n+}\n+\n+// TestOMLXFixtureReplay_ConsumerEndToEnd drives a full ddx work orchestration\n+// path (Runner.RunAgent → agentlib.Run → openai.Provider.ChatStream) against an\n+// httptest double that replays recorded omlx SSE wire traces. This is the\n+// consumer-side smoke test for ddx-agent v0.3.14 — see bead ddx-bbb2d177.\n+//\n+// What this guards against:\n+//\n+//  1. A future ddx-agent bump reintroducing the SSE comment-frame bug (bead\n+//     agent-f237e07b): the happy-path fixture includes `: keep-alive\\n\\n`\n+//     frames between data frames and must NOT surface \"unexpected end of\n+//     JSON input\".\n+//  2. A DDx-side change breaking the omlx dispatch path (e.g., a header or\n+//     request-shape change that trips omlx's parser).\n+//  3. A silent-success regression where the stream is consumed without error\n+//     but the assembled content is empty or malformed — the happy-path assertion\n+//     pins both the final content and finish_reason.\n+//  4. Error-path regressions where a real model-side failure is swallowed by\n+//     the consumer — the error-path fixture emits a malformed data frame and\n+//     asserts the consumer surfaces the error with a specific signature (and\n+//     specifically NOT \"unexpected end of JSON input\", which would mean the\n+//     SSE filter regressed).\n+func TestOMLXFixtureReplay_ConsumerEndToEnd(t *testing.T) {\n+\tfixtureDir := filepath.Join(\"testdata\", \"omlx-wire\")\n+\tcases := []string{\"happy_path.jsonl\", \"error_path.jsonl\"}\n+\n+\tfor _, name := range cases {\n+\t\tname := name\n+\t\tt.Run(name, func(t *testing.T) {\n+\t\t\tfix := loadOMLXFixture(t, filepath.Join(fixtureDir, name))\n+\n+\t\t\tsrv := newOMLXReplayServer(t, fix.Frames)\n+\n+\t\t\tprovider := openai.New(openai.Config{\n+\t\t\t\tBaseURL: srv.URL + \"/v1\",\n+\t\t\t\tAPIKey:  \"test\",\n+\t\t\t\tModel:   \"Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit\",\n+\t\t\t\t// Flavor \"omlx\" pins the capability table to the one used in\n+\t\t\t\t// production for vidar-omlx routing — matches the real\n+\t\t\t\t// dispatch path.\n+\t\t\t\tFlavor: \"omlx\",\n+\t\t\t})\n+\n+\t\t\tr := NewRunner(Config{SessionLogDir: t.TempDir(), TimeoutMS: 10000})\n+\t\t\tr.LookPath = mockLookPath\n+\t\t\tr.AgentProvider = provider\n+\n+\t\t\t// A WorkDir prevents the agent from using the live worktree when\n+\t\t\t// it invokes its built-in read/write/bash tools during the loop.\n+\t\t\tresult, err := r.RunAgent(RunOptions{\n+\t\t\t\tHarness: \"agent\",\n+\t\t\t\tPrompt:  \"smoke test — omlx fixture replay\",\n+\t\t\t\tWorkDir: t.TempDir(),\n+\t\t\t})\n+\t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err, \"RunAgent must not return a plumbing error; failures are reported via result.ExitCode / result.Error\")\n+\n+\t\t\t// The regression signature is always disallowed — on BOTH paths.\n+\t\t\t// v0.3.13 would surface this even for well-formed streams when\n+\t\t\t// keep-alive comment frames were present.\n+\t\t\tif fix.Meta.Expected.ErrorMustNotContain != \"\" {\n+\t\t\t\tassert.NotContains(t, result.Error, fix.Meta.Expected.ErrorMustNotContain,\n+\t\t\t\t\t\"consumer surfaced the v0.3.13 SSE comment-frame regression signature — the sseCommentFilter in ddx-agent v0.3.14+ is missing or broken\")\n+\t\t\t}\n+\n+\t\t\tswitch fix.Meta.Expected.Status {\n+\t\t\tcase \"success\":\n+\t\t\t\tassert.Equal(t, 0, result.ExitCode, \"happy-path fixture must close cleanly; got error=%q\", result.Error)\n+\t\t\t\tassert.Empty(t, result.Error, \"happy-path must produce no error\")\n+\t\t\t\tif want := fix.Meta.Expected.ContentContains; want != \"\" {\n+\t\t\t\t\tassert.Contains(t, result.Output, want,\n+\t\t\t\t\t\t\"happy-path must assemble content from post-keep-alive data frames\")\n+\t\t\t\t}\n+\t\t\tcase \"error\":\n+\t\t\t\tassert.Equal(t, 1, result.ExitCode, \"error-path fixture must report failure via ExitCode=1\")\n+\t\t\t\tassert.NotEmpty(t, result.Error, \"error-path must surface a non-empty error\")\n+\t\t\t\tif want := fix.Meta.Expected.ErrorContains; want != \"\" {\n+\t\t\t\t\tassert.Contains(t, result.Error, want,\n+\t\t\t\t\t\t\"error-path must propagate the underlying parse error\")\n+\t\t\t\t}\n+\t\t\tdefault:\n+\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"fixture %s: unknown expected status %q\", name, fix.Meta.Expected.Status)\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\t})\n+\t}\n+}\n+\n+// TestOMLXFixtureReplay_FixturesExist is a cheap guardrail that fails fast if\n+// the testdata/omlx-wire directory is emptied or renamed. The full replay test\n+// above would also fail in that case, but a dedicated assertion surfaces the\n+// structural problem without the noise of a streaming-test stack trace.\n+func TestOMLXFixtureReplay_FixturesExist(t *testing.T) {\n+\tfor _, name := range []string{\"happy_path.jsonl\", \"error_path.jsonl\"} {\n+\t\tp := filepath.Join(\"testdata\", \"omlx-wire\", name)\n+\t\tinfo, err := os.Stat(p)\n+\t\trequire.NoError(t, err, \"missing fixture %s\", p)\n+\t\tassert.False(t, info.IsDir(), \"%s must be a file\", p)\n+\t\tassert.NotZero(t, info.Size(), \"%s must not be empty\", p)\n+\t}\n+}\ndiff --git a/cli/internal/agent/omlx_smoke_test.go b/cli/internal/agent/omlx_smoke_test.go\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 00000000..fd43c76e\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/cli/internal/agent/omlx_smoke_test.go\n@@ -0,0 +1,66 @@\n+//go:build omlx_smoke\n+\n+// Opt-in live smoke test for the ddx work → ddx-agent → vidar-omlx consumer\n+// path. Unlike the fixture-replay test in omlx_e2e_test.go (which ships in CI\n+// and uses a recorded test double), this test hits a real omlx endpoint and is\n+// intended for manual release verification against a live server. It is gated\n+// by the `omlx_smoke` build tag so the default `go test ./...` never runs it.\n+//\n+// Run:\n+//\n+//\tOMLX_SMOKE_URL=http://vidar:1235/v1\\\n+//\tOMLX_SMOKE_MODEL=Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit\\\n+//\tgo test -tags omlx_smoke -run TestOMLXLiveSmoke -v ./cli/internal/agent\n+//\n+// This covers the same happy-path shape the CI fixture does, but against a\n+// real server — useful for catching provider-side regressions (the omlx\n+// server ships a breaking change, HTTP stack differences, headers, etc.) that\n+// a recorded fixture cannot detect.\n+\n+package agent\n+\n+import (\n+\t\"os\"\n+\t\"testing\"\n+\n+\t\"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent/provider/openai\"\n+\t\"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert\"\n+\t\"github.com/stretchr/testify/require\"\n+)\n+\n+func TestOMLXLiveSmoke(t *testing.T) {\n+\tbaseURL := os.Getenv(\"OMLX_SMOKE_URL\")\n+\tif baseURL == \"\" {\n+\t\tt.Skip(\"OMLX_SMOKE_URL not set — live smoke requires an omlx endpoint\")\n+\t}\n+\tmodel := os.Getenv(\"OMLX_SMOKE_MODEL\")\n+\tif model == \"\" {\n+\t\tt.Fatal(\"OMLX_SMOKE_MODEL is required when OMLX_SMOKE_URL is set\")\n+\t}\n+\n+\tprovider := openai.New(openai.Config{\n+\t\tBaseURL: baseURL,\n+\t\tAPIKey:  os.Getenv(\"OMLX_SMOKE_API_KEY\"),\n+\t\tModel:   model,\n+\t\tFlavor:  \"omlx\",\n+\t})\n+\n+\tr := NewRunner(Config{SessionLogDir: t.TempDir(), TimeoutMS: 120000})\n+\tr.LookPath = mockLookPath\n+\tr.AgentProvider = provider\n+\n+\tresult, err := r.RunAgent(RunOptions{\n+\t\tHarness: \"agent\",\n+\t\tPrompt:  \"Reply with the single word 'ready' and nothing else.\",\n+\t\tWorkDir: t.TempDir(),\n+\t})\n+\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n+\n+\t// The regression signature is the precise error v0.3.13 surfaced when\n+\t// omlx emitted keep-alive SSE comment frames during reasoning warmup.\n+\t// Any bump that reintroduces it must be caught here before it ships.\n+\tassert.NotContains(t, result.Error, \"unexpected end of JSON input\",\n+\t\t\"live omlx produced the v0.3.13 SSE comment-frame regression signature\")\n+\tassert.Equal(t, 0, result.ExitCode, \"live omlx smoke must close cleanly; error=%q\", result.Error)\n+\tassert.NotEmpty(t, result.Output, \"live omlx must return some content\")\n+}\ndiff --git a/cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/README.md b/cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/README.md\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 00000000..1f3cbdfa\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/README.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,61 @@\n+# omlx SSE Wire Fixtures\n+\n+These fixtures drive `cli/internal/agent/omlx_e2e_test.go` — the consumer-side\n+end-to-end smoke test that guards `ddx work` against regressions on the\n+`ddx-agent → vidar-omlx` dispatch path. Context: bead **ddx-bbb2d177**, which\n+was filed after v0.3.14 shipped the `sseCommentFilter` fix for bead\n+**agent-f237e07b** (upstream).\n+\n+## File format\n+\n+Each fixture is a JSONL file with two record kinds:\n+\n+- First line: `{\"kind\":\"meta\", ...}` — describes the capture and the assertion\n+  shape the replay test will enforce (expected status, substrings that must or\n+  must not appear in the assembled content / error).\n+- Remaining lines: `{\"kind\":\"frame\",\"body\":\"<raw SSE body>\"}` — one frame per\n+  record, written verbatim (including `\\n\\n` terminators) to the test HTTP\n+  response in arrival order.\n+\n+Frames include both `: keep-alive` SSE comment frames (the exact shape that\n+triggered the v0.3.13 \"unexpected end of JSON input\" regression) and normal\n+`data: {...}` chunks.\n+\n+## When to regenerate\n+\n+Regenerate fixtures only when real omlx server behavior changes in a way the\n+existing captures no longer represent — for example, a new reasoning-phase\n+frame shape, a header shift, or a chunk-boundary change. Do NOT regenerate\n+just to refresh timestamps.\n+\n+## How to regenerate\n+\n+Use `scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh`, which wraps `ddx agent run` with\n+`AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1` (shipped in ddx-agent v0.3.14) so the full\n+stream is captured to a sidecar JSONL, then reshapes it into the fixture\n+format:\n+\n+```bash\n+scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh \\\n+  --endpoint http://vidar:1235/v1 \\\n+  --model    Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit \\\n+  --prompt   \"Reply with the word ready.\" \\\n+  --name     happy-path \\\n+  --status   success \\\n+  --out      cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/happy_path.jsonl\n+```\n+\n+After the script writes the fixture, hand-edit the `meta.expected` block to\n+pin the `content_contains` / `error_contains` / `finish_reason` assertions for\n+the capture you just took — the script cannot infer those from the wire\n+bytes alone.\n+\n+## Why fixtures live here instead of the upstream agent repo\n+\n+The agent repo (`DocumentDrivenDX/agent`) has its own provider-level SSE\n+tests in `provider/openai/openai_test.go`. Those guard the SDK's handling of\n+SSE framing in isolation. The fixtures in *this* directory guard the DDx\n+consumer — `ddx work` → `Runner.RunAgent` → `agentlib.Run` — against both\n+SDK regressions (a bad ddx-agent bump) and DDx-side regressions (a header or\n+request-shape change that trips omlx's parser). The two layers cover\n+different failure modes and both matter.\ndiff --git a/cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/error_path.jsonl b/cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/error_path.jsonl\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 00000000..3dd1154f\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/error_path.jsonl\n@@ -0,0 +1,6 @@\n+{\"kind\":\"meta\",\"name\":\"error-path\",\"description\":\"omlx-style server sends role delta, a reasoning chunk, then a malformed JSON data frame. b/scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh\n@@ -0,0 +1,108 @@\n+#!/usr/bin/env bash\n+# capture-omlx-fixture.sh — regenerate a testdata/omlx-wire fixture from a\n+# live omlx endpoint. Wraps `ddx agent run` with AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1\n+# (shipped in ddx-agent v0.3.14 — bead agent-f237e07b) and reshapes the wire\n+# dump into the fixture JSONL format the consumer-side smoke test expects.\n+#\n+# Usage:\n+#   scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh \\\n+#     --endpoint http://vidar:1235/v1 \\\n+#     --model    Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit \\\n+#     --prompt   \"Reply with the word ready.\" \\\n+#     --out      cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/happy_path.jsonl\n+#\n+# Required tools: ddx (v0.6.0-alpha22+), jq.\n+#\n+# What the script does:\n+#   1. Runs an isolated ddx agent invocation against the given omlx endpoint\n+#      with AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE=1 AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1 so the whole\n+#      SSE stream is captured to a sidecar wire file.\n+#   2. Extracts every `dir==\"response\"` body chunk in arrival order and emits\n+#      one {\"kind\":\"frame\",\"body\":...} record per chunk.\n+#   3. Prepends a {\"kind\":\"meta\",...} record with the endpoint, model, prompt,\n+#      and a user-supplied expected outcome so the replay test knows what to\n+#      assert.\n+#\n+# Fixtures are checked in. The first\n+// line is the {\"kind\":\"meta\", ...} record; remaining {\"kind\":\"frame\",\"body\":...}\n+// lines are the SSE bodies to stream in order.\n+func loadOMLXFixture(t *testing.T, path string) omlxFixture {\n+\tt.Helper()\n+\tf, err := os.Open(path) // #nosec G304 -- test fixtures are checked in.\n+\trequire.NoError(t, err, \"open fixture %s\", path)\n+\tt.Cleanup(func() { _ = f.Close() })\n+\n+\tvar fix omlxFixture\n+\tscanner := bufio.NewScanner(f)\n+\t// Default 64KB scanner buffer is tight for long reasoning lines.\n+\tscanner.Buffer(make([]byte, 64*1024), 1024*1024)\n+\tfirst := true\n+\tfor scanner.Scan() {\n+\t\tline := strings.TrimSpace(scanner.Text())\n+\t\tif line == \"\" {\n+\t\t\tcontinue\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tvar record struct {\n+\t\t\tKind string          `json:\"kind\"`\n+\t\t\tBody string          `json:\"body\"`\n+\t\t\tRaw  json.RawMessage `json:\"-\"`\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &record); A future ddx-agent bump reintroducing the SSE comment-frame bug (bead\n+//     agent-f237e07b): the happy-path fixture includes `: keep-alive\\n\\n`\n+//     frames between data frames and must NOT surface \"unexpected end of\n+//     JSON input\".\n+//  2. the consumer-side\n+end-to-end smoke test that guards `ddx work` against regressions on the\n+`ddx-agent → vidar-omlx` dispatch path. Context: bead **ddx-bbb2d177**, which\n+was filed after v0.3.14 shipped the `sseCommentFilter` fix for bead\n+**agent-f237e07b** (upstream).\n+\n+## File format\n+\n+Each fixture is a JSONL file with two record kinds:\n+\n+- First line: `{\"kind\":\"meta\", ...}` — describes the capture and the assertion\n+  shape the replay test will enforce (expected status, substrings that must or\n+  must not appear in the assembled content / error).\n+- Remaining lines: `{\"kind\":\"frame\",\"body\":\"<raw SSE body>\"}` — one frame per\n+  record, written verbatim (including `\\n\\n` terminators) to the test HTTP\n+  response in arrival order.\n+\n+Frames include both `: keep-alive` SSE comment frames (the exact shape that\n+triggered the v0.3.13 \"unexpected end of JSON input\" regression) and normal\n+`data: {...}` chunks.\n+\n+## When to regenerate\n+\n+Regenerate fixtures only when real omlx server behavior changes in a way the\n+existing captures no longer represent — for example, a new reasoning-phase\n+frame shape, a header shift, or a chunk-boundary change. Do NOT regenerate\n+just to refresh timestamps.\n+\n+## How to regenerate\n+\n+Use `scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh`, which wraps `ddx agent run` with\n+`AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1` (shipped in ddx-agent v0.3.14) so the full\n+stream is captured to a sidecar JSONL, then reshapes it into the fixture\n+format:\n+\n+```bash\n+scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh \\\n+  --endpoint http://vidar:1235/v1 \\\n+  --model    Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit \\\n+  --prompt   \"Reply with the word ready.\" \\\n+  --name     happy-path \\\n+  --status   success \\\n+  --out      cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/happy_path.jsonl\n+```\n+\n+After the script writes the fixture, hand-edit the `meta.expected` block to\n+pin the `content_contains` / `error_contains` / `finish_reason` assertions for\n+the capture you just took — the script cannot infer those from the wire\n+bytes alone.\n+\n+## Why fixtures live here instead of the upstream agent repo\n+\n+The agent repo (`DocumentDrivenDX/agent`) has its own provider-level SSE\n+tests in `provider/openai/openai_test.go`. Those guard the SDK's handling of\n+SSE framing in isolation. The fixtures in *this* directory guard the DDx\n+consumer — `ddx work` → `Runner.RunAgent` → `agentlib.Run` — against both\n+SDK regressions (a bad ddx-agent bump) and DDx-side regressions (a header or\n+request-shape change that trips omlx's parser). b/scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh\n@@ -0,0 +1,108 @@\n+#!/usr/bin/env bash\n+# capture-omlx-fixture.sh — regenerate a testdata/omlx-wire fixture from a\n+# live omlx endpoint. Wraps `ddx agent run` with AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1\n+# (shipped in ddx-agent v0.3.14 — bead agent-f237e07b) and reshapes the wire\n+# dump into the fixture JSONL format the consumer-side smoke test expects.\n+#\n+# Usage:\n+#   scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh \\\n+#     --endpoint http://vidar:1235/v1 \\\n+#     --model    Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit \\\n+#     --prompt   \"Reply with the word ready.\" \\\n+#     --out      cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/happy_path.jsonl\n+#\n+# Required tools: ddx (v0.6.0-alpha22+), jq.\n+#\n+# What the script does:\n+#   1. Runs an isolated ddx agent invocation against the given omlx endpoint\n+#      with AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE=1 AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1 so the whole\n+#      SSE stream is captured to a sidecar wire file.\n+#   2. Extracts every `dir==\"response\"` body chunk in arrival order and emits\n+#      one {\"kind\":\"frame\",\"body\":...} record per chunk.\n+#   3. Prepends a {\"kind\":\"meta\",...} record with the endpoint, model, prompt,\n+#      and a user-supplied expected outcome so the replay test knows what to\n+#      assert.\n+#\n+# Fixtures are checked in. Regenerate only when the omlx server changes wire\n+# behavior (new frame type, header shift, chunk-size change) in a way the\n+# existing fixtures no longer represent.\n+\n+set -euo pipefail\n+\n+endpoint=\"\"\n+model=\"\"\n+prompt=\"\"\n+out=\"\"\n+name=\"\"\n+status=\"success\"\n+\n+while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do\n+  case \"$1\" in\n+    --endpoint) endpoint=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --model)    model=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --prompt)   prompt=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --out)      out=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --name)     name=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --status)   status=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;  # success | error\n+    -h|--help)\n+      sed -n '2,28p' \"$0\"\n+      exit 0\n+      ;;\n+    *) echo \"unknown arg: $1\" >&2; exit 2 ;;\n+  esac\n+done\n+\n+for v in endpoint model prompt out; do\n+  if [[ -z \"${!v}\" ]]; then\n+    echo \"missing required --$v\" >&2\n+    exit 2\n+  fi\n+done\n+\n+command -v jq >/dev/null || { echo \"jq is required\" >&2; exit 2; }\n+command -v ddx >/dev/null || { echo \"ddx is required\" >&2; exit 2; }\n+\n+wire_file=\"$(mktemp -t omlx-wire.XXXXXX.jsonl)\"\n+trap 'rm -f \"$wire_file\"' EXIT\n+\n+echo \"capturing wire dump from $endpoint → $wire_file\" >&2\n+\n+AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE=1 \\\n+AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1 \\\n+AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_FILE=\"$wire_file\" \\\n+ddx agent run \\\n+  --harness agent \\\n+  --provider vidar-omlx \\\n+  --model \"$model\" \\\n+  --prompt-text \"$prompt\" \\\n+  --timeout 120s \\\n+  >/dev/null || {\n+    echo \"ddx agent run failed — fixture capture incomplete\" >&2\n+    echo \"wire dump (may still be useful): $wire_file\" >&2\n+    exit 1\n+  }\n+\n+[[ -z \"$name\" ]] && name=\"$(basename \"$out\" .jsonl)\"\n+\n+tmp_out=\"$(mktemp -t omlx-fixture.XXXXXX.jsonl)\"\n+trap 'rm -f \"$wire_file\" \"$tmp_out\"' EXIT\n+\n+# Meta record — expected outcome is a hint for the replay test. Authors may\n+# need to hand-edit content_contains / error_contains after inspecting the\n+# captured body.\n+jq -cn \\\n+  --arg name \"$name\" \\\n+  --arg desc \"Captured $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) from $endpoint model=$model prompt=$prompt\" \\\n+  --arg status \"$status\" \\\n+  '{kind:\"meta\", name:$name, description:$desc,\n+    expected:{status:$status, error_must_not_contain:\"unexpected end of JSON input\"}}' \\\n+  >> \"$tmp_out\"\n+\n+# Frame records — every response chunk in capture order. AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE\n+# with STREAM_FULL=1 emits one {\"dir\":\"response\",\"body\":\"…\"} record per read.\n+jq -c 'select(.dir==\"response\" and (.body|type==\"string\") and (.body|length>0))\n+       | {kind:\"frame\", body:.body}' \\\n+  \"$wire_file\" >> \"$tmp_out\"\n+\n+mv \"$tmp_out\" \"$out\"\n+echo \"wrote $(wc -l < \"$out\") records to $out\" >&2","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}
